https://www.ark.ac.uk/elections/fr11.htm "Frustratingly, the detail of the results has been published in two completely different geographical configurations. The turnout was published for each parliamentary copnstituency [sic]... The relative votes for "Yes" and No", meanwhile, were given accoring to the count centres, which more or less combine parliamentary constituencies..." name: 2001 Wards
userNotes: The 1993 Wards, as used in the 2001 Census for statistical purposes.

name: Civil Parishes of Northern Ireland
userNotes: OSNI Baronies and Parishes maps obtained via FOI request to Mapping.Helpdesk@finance-ni.gov.uk, who commented as follows: "The Parish and Baronies dataset was created in 2007 based on the existing Townlands dataset combined with NISRA data for a specific project.  While it was originally envisaged that the dataset would be released as an additional OSNI product for public supply, it proved difficult to verify the exact townlands making up parishes due to conflicts within source material.   The data was developed to a state that was fit-for-purpose at the time, however, OSNI was unable to fully QA the product and so was not considered to be at a sufficient standard to add to our product catalogue.  As customers enquired after the dataset, as a goodwill gesture, we agreed to share it on request, with the understanding that it is not an official product and so we could not stand over its accuracy or completeness.  For the same reason, there is no documentation or analysis of the data to support it.  Therefore, the only additional information we can provide you with is what we have observed from our own review of the data: there are topographical errors, such as sliver polygons, missing attribution and additional fields that could be cleansed out if desired."

Same as 2023 Parliamentary Constituencies. The first election to use these boundaries will be the next Assembly election following the 2022 Assembly election.
